title: Alok Madan
description: A photography portfolio about city life, movement, solitude, and the trace a place leaves after you pass through it.
intro: City rooms, passing light, and the residue left after looking.
introDesktop: A person moves through the city; the city moves back through them. These rooms follow that exchange from first light and public current into pressure, night, residue, and release.
introMobile: City rooms, passing light, and the residue left after looking.
contactBio: Alok Madan photographs cities as lived weather: streets, transit systems, windows, signs, bodies at the edge of the frame, and the quiet pressure of attention. The work follows how public space becomes personal without announcing itself.
contactRepresented: Available for editorial commissions, city and travel features, cultural storytelling, visual research, and long-form photographic sequencing. Select assignments and personal projects are developed from New York and recurring international routes.
disclaimerText: All photographs, sequences, and written material on this site are presented for portfolio review, editorial inquiry, and commission consideration. Images may not be reproduced, distributed, scraped, altered, trained on, or reused without written permission.
